evidence.generatedSwiftBindings: agentType: AgentType? found in sshConnectRemoteServer (line 2988, 3089) and sshStartRemoteServerConnect (line 2992, 3123)

assertions.title: Codex connection unchanged when agent type is nil

steps.action: Review is_non_codex_agent() and ssh connect routing in ffi/ssh.rs
steps.expected: None and Some(AgentType::Codex) both return false from is_non_codex_agent(), routing to existing guided SSH connect path
steps.observed: is_non_codex_agent(None) returns false (line 928 test confirms). is_non_codex_agent(Some(AgentType::Codex)) returns false (line 933 test confirms). When is_non_codex_agent returns false, the code enters the 'else' branch (line 416): 'Codex (or None): existing guided SSH connect path unchanged.' which calls run_guided_ssh_connect() with the exact same parameters as before.

steps.action: Review backward compatibility tests in ffi/ssh.rs
steps.expected: Tests confirm None agent_type follows guided SSH connect path
steps.observed: ssh_start_remote_server_connect_accepts_agent_type_param test (line 964) calls with agent_type: None and asserts Ok result. ssh_connect_remote_server_accepts_agent_type_none test (line 1073) confirms backward compatibility. Both tests pass.

evidence.codeReview: is_non_codex_agent(None) = false → enters existing guided SSH connect path (run_guided_ssh_connect) unchanged. Same code path as pre-mission behavior.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed. VAL-IOS-004: iOS simulator build succeeds with updated bindings containing agentType: AgentType? parameter. VAL-IOS-005: All 992 Rust tests pass; code review confirms None/Codex agent_type routes to unchanged guided SSH connect path.
